Content_Sketch: Scar visible on torso - subject pulls shirt down to hide it
Illustration_Note: Close-up: thin stickman pulling worn shirt down, long scar visible on side torso

Content_Sketch: Weight line: They said it grows back - so you believed them
Illustration_Note: Text overlay: They said it grows back. So you believed them. Dark background

Content_Sketch: Dry field stretching to horizon - your child sits on bare dirt floor
Illustration_Note: Wide shot: cracked earth field, small hut, tiny stickman child sitting on dirt floor

Content_Sketch: Debt collector stands outside your yard - does not knock, just stands
Illustration_Note: Figure standing motionless outside fence, SUBJECT visible through window

Content_Sketch: The phrase it grows back spreads through the village like a fact
Illustration_Note: Two villagers talking, one gestures at own side - landscape of small houses

Content_Sketch: You begin to consider it - not seriously, not yet - but the number stays in your head
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT sitting alone at night, staring at nothing, child sleeping behind

Content_Sketch: Paper is placed on table - handwritten number, no one explains anything
Illustration_Note: POV: table surface, a hand enters frame placing folded paper with number written on it

Content_Sketch: Someone else already took the paper before you - they are gone now
Illustration_Note: Empty chair where another person sat, door slightly open, SUBJECT looking

Content_Sketch: Night bus to the city - last hours with your body still complete
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT sitting on crowded night bus, plastic bag on lap, staring out dark window

Content_Sketch: Silent waiting room - others sit in same posture, no one looks at anyone
Illustration_Note: Small room, 3-4 stickmen sitting apart on plastic chairs, fluorescent light

Content_Sketch: Operating room light turns on - you ask how long until it grows back
Illustration_Note: POV: lying down, harsh surgical lamp overhead, masked figures standing around

Content_Sketch: No one answers - countdown from ten
Illustration_Note: Close-up: SUBJECT face under surgical light, eyes closing, light fading to black

Content_Sketch: You wake up lighter - something is different but you cannot name it
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT lying on thin mattress, bandage on torso, cheap hotel room, dim light

Content_Sketch: You try to stand - body responds wrong - you try to remember what normal felt like - you cannot
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT half-standing, one hand on wall, body tilting, disoriented posture

Content_Sketch: Envelope on bedside table - you do not count it a second time
Illustration_Note: POV: hand holding thin envelope, bills visible but clearly not enough

Content_Sketch: Door opens in the room next door - another person is brought in
Illustration_Note: Hallway view: door opening next room, a figure being guided inside

Content_Sketch: Home again - scar hidden under shirt, money pays half the debt
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT walking back into village, same worn clothes, fading daylight

Content_Sketch: Hands tremble when you carry - back will not straighten anymore
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT trying to lift load in field, arms shaking, hunched posture

Content_Sketch: Standing in your yard - across the road someone adjusts their shirt, edge of a scar visible for one second
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT standing at fence, looking across road at figure pulling shirt edge down

Content_Sketch: A vehicle stops near their house - silence - no one speaks, no one reacts
Illustration_Note: Vehicle stopped on dirt road near house, figure nearby, stillness

Content_Sketch: You pull your shirt down - the scar is still there
Illustration_Note: Mirror of HOOK: SUBJECT pulling shirt down, scar edge disappearing under fabric

Content_Sketch: Somewhere someone is always next
Illustration_Note: Text: And somewhere someone is always next. Fade to black
